# UC-22.59.17 · IMO MSC.428(98) audit-evidence chain — DoC verification + PSC inspection + flag-State audit retrieval ledger

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a coast-guard inspector or auditor visits the ship and asks 'show me proof that you watch your cyber-systems,' we keep all the proof in one place and can hand it over right away. We also check every week that the proof is being collected and is current for every ship.*

---

## Description

Validates that the `audit_evidence` summary index carries weekly rows for every sibling UC in this subcategory and that retrieval-latency SLA (5 minutes for live-incident evidence, 1 hour for historical-audit spans) is honoured during DoC verification, PSC inspection, flag-State audit, and class-society cyber-survey events.

## Value

An auditor or PSC inspector who cannot retrieve evidence within the SLA records the gap as a major non-conformity even if the underlying control is in place. This UC defends the company against avoidable findings by proving the evidence chain is intact.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm every sibling UC writes to `audit_evidence` with the canonical `reg=IMO-MSC-428-98` marker. (2) Wire DoC verification, PSC inspection, flag-State audit, and class-society survey events to a single retrieval workflow. (3) Schedule UC-22.59.17 weekly; route P2 for stale UCs and P3 for retrieval latency outside SLA.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.17` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-428-98-p3"`. Every row pins (event_id, event_type, imo_number, auditor, audit_at, evidence_uc, retrieval_latency_seconds, retrieval_outcome).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A PSC inspector requests evidence of cyber-incident reporting practice during a port call. UC-22.59.17 MUST be able to retrieve UC-22.59.13 evidence within 5 minutes and log retrieval_outcome=success.

### Negative scenario

A flag-State audit requests 24 months of CRAR evidence for a vessel. UC-22.59.17 MUST be able to span the request against `audit_evidence` and log retrieval_outcome=success with retrieval_latency_seconds within SLA.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- All 16 sibling UCs UC-22.59.1 through UC-22.59.16 in production and writing to `audit_evidence`.
- Retention on `audit_evidence` set to ≥ 5 years (typical for IMO + class evidence chain).
- KV Store `imo_doc_verification_lookup`, `iacs_ur_e26_attestation_lookup`, `iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup` populated.

## Step 1 — Canonical evidence marker

Every sibling UC writes a tag `reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=<clause-id>,uc=<uc-id>` in the `marker` field; the search depends on this contract.

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.17: IMO Audit-Evidence Retrieval Ledger]
cron_schedule = 0 8 * * 1
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.17,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-428-98-p3
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Audit-pack export

Provide a Splunk SOAR playbook that, given (imo_number, time-range, sibling-uc-list), exports a single PDF / CSV bundle suitable for handing to the DoC auditor, PSC inspector, or flag-State auditor. This is the operational deliverable of UC-22.59.17.

## Step 4 — Maritime-specific notes

- **PSC inspectors run cyber CICs** (Concentrated Inspection Campaigns). The Paris MoU 2023 CIC routinely demanded cyber-evidence on a vessel-by-vessel basis; this UC is the company's single retrieval point.
- **Class society cyber survey**: each class society performs a periodic E26 / E27 survey on the vessel; this UC is the company's evidence chain for that survey.

## SPL

```spl
index=audit_evidence reg="IMO-MSC-428-98" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
| stats count AS evidence_rows,
        dc(uc) AS distinct_ucs,
        min(_time) AS first_event,
        max(_time) AS last_event
  BY imo_number, uc
| eval freshness_seconds = round(now() - last_event, 0)
| eval freshness_status = case(
    freshness_seconds > 86400, "stale",
    1=1, "fresh")
| append [
    | inputlookup imo_doc_verification_lookup
    | eval freshness_status = "context"
    | table imo_number ship_name doc_verification_due_at freshness_status]
| where freshness_status != "fresh"
| sort 0 -freshness_seconds
| table imo_number ship_name uc evidence_rows distinct_ucs first_event last_event freshness_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Sibling-UC freshness matrix (vessel × UC). (Panel 2) Audit-evidence row volume per UC over 90 days. (Panel 3) Retrieval-latency histogram (live vs historical). (Panel 4) Audit-event timeline (DoC + PSC + flag-State + class).

## Known False Positives

1. **Newly onboarded vessel**: a vessel added <30 days ago will not yet carry historic audit-evidence rows; honour the onboarding grace window. 2. **Subcategory-level audit gap**: every UC must write to `audit_evidence` with the canonical marker; if a sibling UC is broken, this UC will (correctly) flag the gap.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_17_exceptions` with the Company Security Officer documented approval.

## References

- [IMO Resolution MSC.428(98) Paragraph 3](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/Resolution%20MSC.428(98).pdf)
- [Paris MoU PSC 2023 Concentrated Inspection Campaign on Cyber Risk Management](https://www.parismou.org/inspections-risk/library-faq/cic)
- [ISM Code §12 Company verification, review and evaluation](https://www.imo.org/en/OurWork/HumanElement/Pages/ISMCode.aspx)
